refnamediv.refpurpose: Store data at the server only if the CAS token matches

refentry.refsect1:
&reftitle.description;
boolMemcache::cas stringkey mixedvar intflag intexpire intcas
boolmemcache_cas Memcachememcache stringkey mixedvar intflag intexpire intcas
Memcache::cas performs a "check and set" operation: the item var is written to the server under key only if its CAS (compare-and-swap) token on the server still matches the cas value supplied. The CAS token is a unique value that the server associates with the item and that changes every time the item is modified, so a mismatch means another client has updated the item in the meantime and the write is rejected. This makes it possible to update an item safely without overwriting concurrent changes.
The current CAS token of an item is retrieved with Memcache::get by passing a variable as the cas parameter (the fourth argument of the procedural form, third of the object form), which is filled by reference with the token. That token is then passed back to Memcache::cas.

&reftitle.parameters;
key
The key that will be associated with the item.
var
The variable to store. Strings and integers are stored as is, other types are stored serialized.
flag
Use MEMCACHE_COMPRESSED to store the item compressed (uses zlib).
expire
Expiration time of the item. If it's equal to zero, the item will never expire. Unix timestamp or a number of seconds starting from current time may be used, but in the latter case the number of seconds may not exceed 2592000 (30 days).
cas
The CAS token previously obtained for this item through Memcache::get. The store is only performed if this token still matches the value the server holds for the item.
